creator: timur@FreeBSD.org
text:
You are not the first one, who reports this problem. But I can't reproduce
it - in my environment everything installs correctly into the non-standard
prefix.

I'm pretty sure that there is subtle difference that makes such a big
change, but can't' spot it.

As for the LOCALBASE - it's basically incorrect to pass it during the build
stage, only PREFIX should be used. LOCALBASE is for installed software only.

Do you use some specific build environment, like poudriere or tinderbox?
Cause I use just plain FreeBSD installation for testing.


On Wed, May 29, 2013 at 10:43 PM, Daniel Hagerty <hag@linnaean.org> wrote:

>     How can I help you reproduce this?  It's pretty straight forward
> here.  There doesn't seem to be anything communicating LOCALBASE to
> the port.
>
